channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: James Munene Thuku Urges Government to Address School Fees and Traffic Issues in Roysambu
item.description: During the burial of his father, Mzee Eshbon Thuku Kwenya, Roysambu politician James Munene Thuku brought up pressing issues affecting residents of the constituency. Thuku appealed directly to Deputy ...

item.title: Debate Over AI Use in Commonwealth Short Story Prize Submissions
item.description: The Commonwealth Short Story Prize has been embroiled in controversy after Jamir Nazir’s winning entry for the Caribbean region, 'The Serpent in the Grove', faced accusations of being AI-generated. Th...

item.title: Lagos Government Strengthens Oversight to Improve Revenue Collection Across 80 Agencies
item.description: The Lagos State government don step up monitoring and supervision across 80 of its parastatals and government-owned agencies as part of reforms to boost revenue, promote transparency, and improve gove...

item.title: Alberta to hold non-binding vote on potential secession, Smith aims to navigate political pressures
item.description: Alberta Premier Danielle Smith announced that the province will hold a province-wide vote in October to decide whether Alberta should remain part of Canada or whether the government should initiate th...

item.title: The 1957 AC Ace-Bristol Roadster: Iconic British Sports Car and Shelby Cobra Precursor
item.description: The 1957 AC Ace-Bristol Roadster stands as one of the most celebrated British sports cars of the post-war period. Manufactured by AC Cars, this model gained legendary status when paired with the Brist...

item.title: Reunert sees low local infrastructure activity despite government plans
item.description: Reunert, a major supplier of power cables to Eskom, has reported a drop in operating profit during the six months to March 31, 2025, largely due to low infrastructure investment in South Africa. The c...

item.title: Carpio: Philippine courts not needed for ICC arrest of Bato dela Rosa
item.description: Retired Senior Associate Justice Antonio Carpio clarified that local Philippine courts are not required for the enforcement of the International Criminal Court (ICC) arrest warrant issued against Sena...

item.title: Tiruchi Collector Conducts Safety Inspection of School Vehicles
item.description: On Friday, District Collector V. Saravanan carried out a special inspection camp for vehicles belonging to private educational institutions at Sri Vignesh Vidyalaya in Koothur, Manachanallur taluk, Ti...

item.title: Life Along the Seine in 1967: Parisian Leisure and Culture
item.description: During the 1960s, the banks of the Seine in Paris offered a serene retreat for locals amid the city's post-war modernization. In 1967, this area exemplified the French concept of 'joie de vivre,' a ca...

item.title: How Copyright Laws and Proprietary Software Restrict Consumer Repairs in the U.S.
item.description: In the United States, repairing consumer electronics and machinery has become increasingly difficult due to proprietary software and intellectual property restrictions. Many everyday items, from smart...

item.title: Increasing Number of Canadian Parents Decline Vitamin K Shots for Newborns, Raising Health Concerns
item.description: In recent years, an increasing number of Canadian parents are choosing not to have their newborns receive vitamin K injections, which are standard preventative treatments to reduce the risk of severe ...

item.title: Trump administration releases new UFO files including photos and military videos
item.description: The Trump Administration has made public a fresh set of UFO-related documents, including new photographs, intelligence reports, and 46 previously unseen videos, following requests from Congress. These...

item.title: Pakistan's CDF Munir visits Tehran to advance Iran-US peace negotiations
item.description: Chief of Defence Forces (CDF) and Chief of the Army Staff, Field Marshal Syed Asim Munir, has left for Tehran on an official visit amid Pakistan's active role in mediating between the United States an...

item.title: AI Tools Helping Kenyan Traders Make Smarter Forex Decisions
item.description: In Nairobi and across Kenya, artificial intelligence (AI) is steadily transforming how forex trading is done. In the past, only hedge funds in major financial hubs like New York had access to sophisti...

item.title: Underground abortion networks grow in Dar es Salaam amid strict legal restrictions
item.description: In Dar es Salaam, strict laws against abortion have pushed many women into a hidden and risky underground market. This informal network involves brokers, pharmacies, and unregistered providers who ope...

item.title: Eastern Cape Remains South Africa’s Most Murder-Prone Province Despite Drop in National Crime
item.description: Despite a general decline in violent crime across South Africa, the Eastern Cape continues to have the highest murder rate in the country, with 14.3 murders per 100,000 people, nearly double that of G...

item.title: Tanzania launches national waste recycling program to protect environment and generate youth employment
item.description: Tanzania imeanza mpango mkubwa wa kuendeleza sekta ya kuchakata taka ili kupunguza mabadiliko ya tabianchi na pia kutoa ajira kwa vijana wengi. Serikali imetangaza mpango wa kitaifa wa kuchakata taka ...

item.title: Rodrigo Duterte to remain in ICC detention pending trial for crimes against humanity
item.description: Former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte will continue to be held in detention at The Hague as he awaits the start of his trial at the International Criminal Court (ICC) over allegations of crimes ...

item.title: Tim Hortons Announces $43 Million Renovation Project for Vancouver Island Stores
item.description: Tim Hortons has announced a significant $43 million investment aimed at renovating its Vancouver Island locations. The initiative is part of the chain's broader commitment to modernizing its presence ...

item.title: Mobile Surgical Unit Brings Specialist Healthcare Closer to Communities
item.description: Dar es Salaam — Tanzania has taken a big step to improve healthcare access for wananchi in remote and underserved areas with the launch of the country's first mobile surgical theatre by Muhimbili Nati...
